rulings.Case Title: SECURITY NATIONAL VS STATE OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Augment The Administrative Record Per Ccp 1094.5(E) And Consider Previously Unavailable Evidence At The Writ Hearing; Or In The Alternative, Enter Judgment For Remand Per Ccp 1094.5(E)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 5, 2012, Line 1. PETITIONER SECURITY NATIONAL GUARANTY INC Motion To Augment The Administrative Record Per Ccp 1094.5(E) And Consider Previously Unavailable Evidence At The Writ Hearing; Or In The Alternative, Enter Judgment For Remand Per Ccp 1094.5(E). Hearing required. The hearing will be at 11:15am. The court contemplates that there will be two further specially set hearings of approximately an hour an a half each beginning at 1:30 later this month. At the hearing on June 5 the court intends to hear argument on the motions to augment and correct the administrative record and on the issues of the water and natural resources issues. The parties are directed to meet and confer regarding suitable dates June 11 or later (excluding June 18) for the two subsequent hearings and the issues to be discussed at those hearings. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILSON POWELL et al VS. DOUGLAS R. LIVINGSTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 5, 2012, Line 3. PLAINTIFF WILSON POWELL, PACIFIC SIGNALING SYSTEMS, INC. Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman as follows: Granted as to interrogatories 24, 31, 38 and 45, denied as to interrogatories 25-30, 32-37, 39-44, and 46-51, and granted, in part as to interrogatory 52. Lexington shall provide the names and addresses in the claim file of the insureds for each claim number identified in response to interrogatories 24, 31, 38, and 45. Lexington need not ascertain if the addresses in the claim file are current. However, unless and until non-party insureds provide written authorization for disclosure of their claim file to plaintiffs in response to a letter which must be agreed to by the parties or approved by the Court before it is sent, plaintiffs should not contact the non-party insureds except through the letter which must be agreed to by the parties of approved by the Court. Additionally, plaintiffs shall not disclose the name or address of any non-party insured to any third party or use the name or address of any non-party insured except for purposes of this litigation. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: FRED G. MEIS ESQ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Fred Meis, Kerrin Meis (Amended) Petition For Revocation Of Assignment For Discharge Of Assignee
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 5, 2012, Line 20. Petitioners Fred Meis, Kerrin Meis (Amended) Petition For Revocation Of Assignment For Discharge Of Assignee. Hearing required. Petitoners contested the following tentative ruling: Denied. Petitioners are not entitled to equitable relief to revoke the irrevocable Assignment for the Benefit Creditors. Since the filing of the petition, Respondent has provided Petitioners with an accounting and Respondent offered to turn over to the client files still in its possession. The court encourages the parties to meet and confer in an effort to reach an agreement to conclude all of the Respondent's rights and duties under the Assignment. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY WESTMAN VS. ROBERT SHAPIRO DR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on tUESday, June 5, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF WENDY WESTMAN, RN'S Motion For A Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)
